dial.summary: Net macro score +2 (4 supportive: Global M2 $102.4T on confirmed 4-week uptrend per StreetStats 4-bank aggregate July 17, Fed balance sheet $6.74T flat per H.4.1 week ended ~July 15 with next release July 24, BTC funding +0.01% near-zero, stablecoin dominance 13.34% in continued 30-day descent from ~14.4% mid-June; 2 neutral: ISM Manufacturing 53.3% above-50-but-falling with July data due August 3, 2s10s +0.37% positive-flat on 4-week basis; 2 headwinds: DXY 100.72 marginally above the 100 threshold on flat 4-week trend—weakened only 0.09% over 30 days, real yields 2.32% on July 17–18 elevated and rising +0.14pp from ~2.18% on June 17); BTC.D 56.3% firm, TOTAL ~$2.28T; the July 28–29 FOMC hold at 3.5–3.75%—and Chair Warsh's acknowledgment of June CPI (3.5% YoY) and soft PPI as validating the disinflationary pipeline—is the primary catalyst to compress DXY below 100 and real yields toward 2%, which would flip both remaining headwinds and reinforce BTC Accumulation into Q3; July 30 Q2 GDP advance/June PCE and BOE/BOJ decisions are the secondary macro tests.

indicators.name: Global Liquidity (Global M2)
indicators.note: StreetStats 4-bank aggregate (Fed/ECB/BOJ/PBOC) at $102,442B ($102.4T) as of July 17, 2026—updated from prior run's MacroMicro May 2026 figure of $102.7T; the StreetStats series reflects more current data with slightly different exchange-rate methodology accounting for the ~$0.3T gap; 4-week trend up: three-month dollar-denominated Global M2 change was +1.70% through end-May, implying approximately +0.57% over 30 days—above the 0.5% flat threshold; US M2 rose from $22,686B (March) to $22,804B (April 2026) at ~+$118B/month per TradingEconomics, consistent with a rising global aggregate; YoY money-supply growth at a multi-year high per Mises Institute analysis; status supportive (4-week uptrend confirmed, YoY growth positive); next MacroMicro/StreetStats June 2026 aggregate expected mid-to-late August given 6–8 week reporting lag.

indicators.name: ISM Manufacturing PMI
indicators.note: June 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI confirmed at 53.3%, released July 1—sixth consecutive month of expansion but 0.7pp below May's 54.0%; no July 2026 data—next ISM Manufacturing PMI releases Monday August 3, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET (within current 14-day calendar window); 4-week trend down (May 54.0% → June 53.3%); new orders 56.0% (-0.8pp), production 52.2% (-2.1pp), employment 49.7% (contracting), prices paid 73.0% (-9.1pp, largest single-month drop since July 2022, consistent with June CPI 3.5% YoY and soft PPI disinflationary signal); status neutral (above 50 but falling—supportive requires above 50 AND rising; headwind requires below 48 and falling); value unchanged this run—no new ISM data until August 3.

indicators.name: DXY (US Dollar Index)
indicators.note: ICE DXY at ~100.72 intraday July 20, 2026 (opened at 100.81; July 17 close was 100.7578 per TradingEconomics); 4-week trend flat: DXY has weakened approximately 0.09% over the past month (TradingEconomics 30-day data), implying a ~June 20 reference level of ~100.81—a change of only -0.09 points over 30 days, well below the 0.5% flat threshold; post-FOMC June 17 analysis noted DXY near 101.17 before pulling back, consistent with the modest 30-day weakening trend; status headwind (ICE DXY at 100.72 remains above the 100 headwind threshold; a sustained close below 100 is required to flip to neutral—the July 28–29 FOMC hold with dovish Warsh language on June CPI (3.5% YoY) disinflation progress is the primary near-term catalyst; over the past 12 months DXY is up 2.31% per TradingEconomics reflecting the lingering rate differential).

indicators.name: Real Yields (US10Y - CPI)
indicators.note: 10-year TIPS real yield (DFII10) at 2.32% as of July 17–18, 2026 (MacroRadar latest reading 2.32%; TradingEconomics confirms 2.31% on July 17; using 2.32% as the most recent print; updated from prior run's 2.29%); 4-week trend up: DFII10 rose approximately +0.14pp from ~2.18% on June 17 (post-FOMC) to 2.32%—well above the 5bp flat threshold; June CPI at 3.5% YoY and soft June PPI are compressing nominal 10Y yields (4.55% on July 17), but breakevens have moved in tandem, limiting real yield compression; status headwind (above 2% and rising on 4-week basis—a sustained move to or below 2.00% is required to flip from headwind to neutral); July 30 Q2 GDP advance and June PCE monthly are the next significant tests of whether disinflation is accelerating enough to compress real yields materially.

indicators.name: 2s10s Yield Curve
indicators.note: 10-year Treasury at ~4.55% and 2-year at ~4.18% as of July 17, 2026 (Advisor Perspectives/ETF DB confirmed: 10Y = 4.55%, spread = +0.37%, implying 2Y = 4.18%; July 10: 10Y 4.56%, 2Y 4.21%, spread +0.35%; July 2: 10Y 4.49%, 2Y 4.14%, spread +0.35%); value updated from prior run's +0.41%—revision reflects 2Y at 4.18% vs prior source's 4.15%, a 3bp difference between data providers on the July 17 close; 4-week trend flat: spread was approximately +0.39% on June 17 (post-FOMC), now +0.37%, a -2bp change well within the 5bp flat threshold; status neutral (positive spread well clear of the -50bps deep-inversion headwind threshold, not steepening on a 30-day basis—supportive requires positive AND steepening; the July 28–29 FOMC hold with dovish Warsh language could steepen the curve; July 31 ECI Q2 is the next major test of wage-driven services inflation pressures).

indicators.name: Fed Balance Sheet
indicators.note: H.4.1 balance sheet: most recent publicly indexed data is $6,736B for week ended July 8, 2026 (H.4.1 dated July 9 per ycharts/Fed website); prior run cited $6,743,028M ($6.74T) for week ended July 15 (H.4.1 dated July 16)—both round to $6.74T; the July 16 H.4.1 has not yet been indexed in public search as of July 20; next H.4.1 release Thursday July 24, 2026 for week ending July 23; 4-week comparison: week ended ~June 18 estimated ~$6.71T; 4-week change ~+$25–30B / +0.37–0.45%, within the flat threshold; year-over-year: ~$73–80B above year-ago levels per Fed data; status supportive (balance sheet flat with no acceleration of QT, no aggressive reserve drain, year-over-year growth modestly positive).

indicators.name: BTC Funding Rates (perps avg)
indicators.note: BTC perpetual funding rate held at +0.01% per 8-hour interval—value unchanged from prior run; live data from CoinGlass, coinalyze, and Bitcoin Magazine Pro all returned HTTP 403 this run and could not be refreshed; indicator added to this run's skipped list; with BTC.D at 56.3% and TOTAL at ~$2.28T, the prior near-zero funding reading is consistent with spot-driven accumulation rather than leveraged long positioning; status supportive (prior reading well below the 0.04% overheating threshold; near-zero funding during stable TOTAL market cap is consistent with healthy BTC Accumulation dynamics; live refresh will be attempted next run).

indicators.name: Stablecoin Dominance
indicators.note: Stablecoin market cap ~$305B against total crypto market cap ~$2.28T as of July 20, 2026 (search-aggregated data: 13.34% share, down from prior run's 13.49%; USDT $186.35B / 59.22% of stablecoin market, USDC $74.89B / 23.80%; BTC.D 56.3%, BTC market cap ~$1.29T); 30-day trend down: dominance was ~14.4% in mid-June, 13.49% in the prior run, and 13.34% now—a -1.06pp decline over 30 days, clear continuation of the downtrend; declining stablecoin dominance alongside stable-to-rising TOTAL ($2.28T, up from $2.26T prior) indicates capital staying in crypto (primarily BTC) rather than rotating to cash; status supportive (30-day downtrend intact; headwind condition requires rising stablecoin dominance while TOTAL crypto market cap also rises—this does not apply as TOTAL is stable-to-slightly-up).

upcoming.title: Existing Home Sales (June 2026)
upcoming.note: National Association of Realtors (NAR) Existing Home Sales for June 2026—closings on previously owned homes reflecting mortgage lock-in dynamics in a still-elevated rate environment (10Y ~4.55%, TIPS real yield 2.32%); released within the FOMC blackout (July 18–30); one trading day before the ECB rate decision on July 23, which will set cross-asset context for the July 28–29 FOMC.

upcoming.title: ECB Rate Decision (July 2026)
upcoming.note: ECB Governing Council decision at 13:45 CET (7:45 AM ET), press conference at 14:30 CET (8:30 AM ET)—July is a non-projection meeting (no updated Staff Economic Projections); ECB deposit facility rate at 2.25% (raised 25bps at the June 11 meeting); markets price a high probability of a hold at July 23; Lagarde's press conference tone is the primary market driver given the non-projection format; will assess whether global disinflation (US CPI 3.5% YoY, soft US PPI) and commodity easing reduce urgency for further action; ECB guidance sets cross-asset context for the July 28–29 FOMC.

upcoming.title: Initial Jobless Claims (week ended July 19, 2026)
upcoming.note: Weekly UI claims at 8:30 AM ET—same morning as ECB decision; last jobless claims print before the July 28–29 FOMC rate decision (announced Wednesday July 29 at 2:00 PM ET); two consecutive weeks above 240K would materially shift rate-cut pricing entering the Wednesday announcement; the ECB + claims combination on July 23 is the most consequential macro morning before the FOMC meeting begins July 28.

upcoming.title: S&P Global Flash US PMI / New Home Sales (July 2026)
upcoming.note: S&P Global Preliminary (Flash) US Manufacturing and Services PMI for July 2026 at ~9:45 AM ET—first comprehensive business activity read for Q3, released four days before the July 28–29 FOMC; a manufacturing flash PMI above 53 consistent with the June ISM (53.3%) would signal expansion broadening into Q3; services PMI is watched for sticky wage-driven services inflation; Census Bureau New Home Sales (June 2026) also at 10:00 AM ET; among the last major indicators the Committee will assess before the FOMC meeting begins July 28.

upcoming.title: Advance Durable Goods Orders (June 2026)
upcoming.note: Census Bureau Advance Monthly Durable Goods for June 2026 at 8:30 AM ET—core capital goods orders (ex-defense, ex-aircraft) are the key business investment sub-component; last significant growth indicator the Committee sees before the two-day FOMC meeting begins July 28; a solid capex print alongside the soft CPI/PPI backdrop would reinforce the soft-landing case for a hold at 3.5–3.75%; weak orders could add downside risk to the Committee's growth outlook.
